verdict.answer_first: A and B both answer in sentence 1: A says "start with a plain in-memory cache"; B says "default to a plain in-memory cache." A is slightly sharper.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits cleanly to "in-memory unless..." while A is also decisive but expands into a broader survey and follow-up question.
verdict.fabrication: Both include uncited performance figures; A says "~100–1000× slower," B says "~0.1–1ms" and "nanosecond reads."
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it names concrete library choices like "Caffeine," "ristretto," "lru-cache," and "MemoryCache."
